candidate_summary: HOKULEA-CREW: distributed multi-agent coordinator inspired by Polynesian Hōkūleʻa wayfinding — N specialist agents emit bearing-reports (cue_id, confidence, vote_value), a helmsman aggregator maps reports into a 32-house discrete consensus heading, conflicts trigger watch-change agent replacement, and an apprentice agent distills master's bearing-distillation behavior.

results.name: Beyond Majority Voting LLM Aggregation (2510.01499)
results.description: LLM aggregation via Optimal Weight + Inverse Surprising Popularity beyond majority voting using first-order + second-order information.
keyword_matches: distributed crew specialist roles (weak — aggregation across agents)
results.rationale: Higher-order voting aggregation is functionally close to the 32-house consensus heading aggregator (both aggregate distributed votes into a single discrete output). Semantically aligned with consensus aggregation but lacks watch-change conflict-trigger and apprentice distillation; conceptually maps to aggregator subcomponent.

results.name: Consensus Trap Multi-Agent LLM Token-Level (2604.17139)
results.description: Token-level round-robin multi-agent collaboration with sequential interleaved generation in shared auto-regressive context.
keyword_matches: distributed crew specialist roles (weak — multi-agent collaboration)
results.rationale: Token-level round-robin is a form of multi-agent coordination but differs substantially from bearing-report + discrete 32-house aggregator + watch-change. Round-robin is not the same as watch-change conflict-triggered replacement. Sequential interleaving operates per-token rather than per-bearing-report. Not a strong functional match to the specific aggregator structure.

results.name: Democratic Multi-Agent AI Voting-Based Council
results.description: Voting-based council pattern with specialist agents + coordinator aggregator yielding consensus.
keyword_matches: per-agent specialty bearing report tuple (partial — voting)
results.rationale: Voting-based council with specialist agents + coordinator aggregator is structurally analogous to HOKULEA's helmsman aggregator + per-agent bearing reports. Maps directly onto the aggregator + voting mechanism. Lacks 32-house discreteness and watch-change conflict-trigger, but the core voting/aggregation paradigm matches.

results.name: Consensus Protocols Multi-Agent Systems 2026 Fast.io
results.description: Consensus protocol guide for multi-agent — voting + thresholding + conflict resolution patterns.
results.rationale: Consensus protocols with voting + thresholding + watch-change-on-conflict patterns cover the core HOKULEA mechanism: aggregation, threshold-triggered conflict response (watch-change), and distributed crew roles. Functional twin to the conflict-trigger coordination paradigm.

results.name: Structured Agent Distillation LLM Agents (2505.13820)
results.description: Structured span-level distillation segmenting reasoning + action spans for fine-grained agent distillation.
results.rationale: Structured agent distillation IS the apprentice-distillation channel for capability transmission in HOKULEA. Maps to the apprentice agent imitating master's bearing-distillation behavior — same artefact (distillation) + same effect (capability transmission via span/reasoning imitation).

results.name: Multi-Agent Collaboration Mechanisms Survey (2501.06322)
results.description: Survey of multi-agent collaboration — broadcast/relay/report networks + blackboards + role-based strategy.
keyword_matches: per-agent specialty bearing report tuple (partial — report networks)
results.rationale: Survey of multi-agent collaboration covers role-based + blackboards + report networks — the same paradigm as HOKULEA's distributed specialists + bearing-reports + shared crew-state blackboard. Acknowledged by candidate as prior art differing only via 32-house discrete + watch-change additions.

results.name: Self-Organizing LLM Agents (2603.28990)
results.description: Self-organizing multi-agent LLM with autonomous role-selection + sequential observation outperforming hardcoded structures.
results.rationale: Self-organizing agents with autonomous role-selection is conceptually similar to watch-change (re-assignment of roles) and to distributed specialist crew roles. Slightly lower functional match because autonomous role-selection differs from conflict-triggered deputy replacement, but the role-restructuring mechanism is shared.

results.name: On-Policy Distillation Thinking Machines Lab
results.description: On-policy distillation matches RL reasoning performance at fraction of cost.
results.rationale: On-policy distillation is a distillation method for capability transmission; functionally maps to the apprentice imitation channel. Same artefact (distillation) and same effect (capability transmission via student imitating teacher). Borderline functional twin at 0.70.

decision_rationale: 7 of 8 results independently meet at least one hit threshold (sem>=0.7, func>=0.7, or kw>=2). Five results (1, 3, 4, 5, 6) score func>=0.70 — each maps to a distinct subcomponent of HOKULEA-CREW (higher-order voting aggregator, voting-council coordinator, consensus protocols with watch-change-on-conflict, structured agent distillation, multi-agent role+blackboard survey). The apprentice-distillation subcomponent has two close twins (results 5 and 8). The 32-house aggregator + helmsman + watch-change combination is fully covered by the union of prior-art clusters; the novelty reduces to a re-labelling/recombination of mainstream patterns. Result 2 (token-level round-robin) is the only marginal miss. Multi-cluster coverage across aggregator, conflict-resolution, distillation, and role-based collaboration confirms FAIL.
